这是这家搜索巨头今天就News Showcase发布的几项公告之一，该计划向发布商付款（最初承诺投入10亿美元），以许可其内容在Google新闻中使用新格式。到目前为止，Google News Showcase已在德国，巴西，阿根廷，加拿大，法国，英国和澳大利亚等国家/地区启动，在某些情况下，这些市场以前曾面临法律挑战和反托拉斯审查。
 谷歌表示，它将向参与发行的发行商付款，以“为News Showcase用户提供对付费墙内容的有限访问权”。但是，这些用户仍然需要直接向发布商注册，Google表示这将为他们提供建立联系的方式。 （Facebook还一直在尝试展示付费墙内容的方法，在这种情况下，是通过将Facebook帐户与新闻订阅相关联。）
   News Showcase的主要格式本质上是故事面板，Google表示正在引入一个新面板，允许发布者每天挑选他们最重要的故事。这些面板将显示给关注这些发布者的用户。
  Google还将News Showcase引入了新的设备和渠道。它始于Android上的Google新闻，现在也可以在iOS上使用，并计划扩展到news.google.com网站和“很快发现”。它说，自从10月启动以来，合作伙伴的数量已经翻了一番-参与该计划的近400家出版商名单包括法国的Le Monde，Courrier International，L'Obs，Le Figaro，Libération和L'Express等新名字。以及阿根廷的Página12，La Gaceta和ElDía。
 该公司写道：“随着2020年即将结束，见证News Showcase的进展以及全球发行商和读者的热情令人振奋。” “我们将在构建功能和扩展产品时继续参与并纳入反馈，以增加新闻合作伙伴的未来可持续性。”
note_en:
That’s one of  several announcements that the search giant made today about News Showcase, the program where it pays publishers ( with $1 billion committed initially) to license their content for a new format in Google News. So far, Google News Showcase has launched in such countries as Germany, Brazil, Argentina, Canada, France, U.K. and Australia — in several cases, those are markets where it’s previously faced legal challenges and antitrust scrutiny.
 Google says it will be paying participating publishers to provide “limited access to paywalled content for News Showcase users.” Those users will, however, still need to register directly with the publishers, which Google says will give them a way to build a relationship. (Facebook has also been experimenting with ways to present paywalled content, in its case by  linking Facebook accounts to news subscriptions.)
   The main News Showcase format is essentially story panel, and Google says it’s introducing a new panel allowing publishers to curate a daily selection of their most important stories. Those panels will be shown to users who follow those publishers.
  Google is also bringing the News Showcase to new devices and channels. It started out on Google News on Android and is now available on iOS as well, with plans to expand to the news.google.com website and Discover soon. And it says it has doubled the number of partners since the launch in October — the list of nearly 400 publishers participating in the program includes new names like Le Monde, Courrier International, L’Obs, Le Figaro, Libération and L’Express in France, plus Página12, La Gaceta and El Día in Argentina.
 “As 2020 comes to a close, it is heartening to witness the progress of News Showcase and the enthusiasm from both publishers and readers around the world,” the company writes. “We will continue to engage and incorporate feedback as we build out features and grow the product to add to the future sustainability of our news partners.”
title: 谷歌表示其新闻展示将增加免费访问付费墙的故事
title_en: Google Says Its News Showcase Will Add Free Access To Paywalled Stories

note:
Salesforce联合创始人兼首席执行官马克·贝尼奥夫（Marc Benioff）对他最近的一次收购没有遗漏任何话。 “这是天上的火柴。贝尼奥夫在一份声明中说：“ Salesforce和Slack将共同塑造企业软件的未来，并改变所有人在全数字，可从任何地方工作的世界中的工作方式。”
 Slack的首席执行官斯图尔特·巴特菲尔德（Stewart Butterfield）毫不逊色于他的未来老板。随着软件在每个组织的绩效中扮演越来越重要的角色，我们的愿景是降低复杂性，增强功能和灵活性，并最终实现更大程度的一致性和组织敏捷性。就我个人而言，我认为这是软件历史上最具战略意义的组合，我迫不及待想开始前进。” Butterfield在一份声明中说。
  每个公司的每个员工都需要进行沟通，这是Slack可以赋予他们的能力。而且，它还促进了与客户和合作伙伴的外部交流，这对于Salesforce及其产品家族之类的公司来说应该是非常有用的。
 最终，Slack可以接受了。自进入2020年以来，它的市值已损失了约40％。考虑一下，在其最新的收益报告之后，该公司的价值缩水了16％，而在与Salesforce达成交易之前，该公司的每股价值仅比其直接上市参考价高出几美元。截至2020年7月31日的两个季度，Slack的净亏损为1.476亿美元，这是Slack令人鼓舞的公开估值以及通往盈利之路的曲折之路，它是此类收购的长期目标。这里唯一的惊喜是价格。
 根据Yahoo和Google Finance的说法，Slack目前的估值刚刚超过250亿美元，鉴于其在下班后的价格变动非常适度，这意味着市场对该公司的定价有些有效。 Slack较该交易公布前的估值上涨了约48％。
  这项新交易还使Salesforce与其主要竞争对手和曾经的朋友Microsoft处于同等水平，并且在竞争中更具竞争优势，后者的Teams产品直接挑战了市场上的Slack。微软过去以收购Salesforce现在支付的一小部分价格收购了Slack，但微软已将Teams作为最近几个季度的主要优先事项，不愿将企业软件市场的任何部分割让给另一家公司。
 至少在最初，真正使Slack与众不同的是它与其他企业软件集成的能力。当您将这些与机器人（这些智能的数字助手）结合在一起时，该公司可能会为Salesforce客户提供一个集中的工作场所，而无需改变重点，因为他们需要做的所有事情都可以在Slack中完成。 
今天的交易是在Salesforce在2016年以7.5亿美元收购Quip之后达成的。 Quip为SaaS巨头带来了一种社交共享文档的方式，当与Slack收购相结合时，Salesforce可以比其内部选择Chatter讲述更强大的社交故事，这是对企业社交的早期尝试，从未真正流行。
 值得注意的是，Salesforce在2016年对Twitter感兴趣，据报道微软对Slack于同年，但是当股东反对时，Salesforce最终放弃了这一交易，不想与这个社交平台有争议的一面打交道。
 Slack成立于2013年，但其起源可以追溯到一家成立于2009年的在线多人游戏公司Glitch。尽管该游戏最终失败了，但该初创公司在建立公司的过程中开发了内部消息传递系统，该公司后来得以发展进入松弛状态。
 该公司的历史性增长帮助Slack筹集了超过10亿美元的私人资金，在去年上市之前，获得了令人印象深刻的70亿美元估值。但是，尽管从毛刺到独角兽的故事看起来很简单，但Slack一直面临着来自微软，思科，Facebook，谷歌甚至Asana和Monday.com等公司的激烈竞争。
 对于Slack来说，通往公开市场的道路充满了炒作和过大的期望。该公司是著名的，或者像一家企业软件公司一样著名。当时感觉它的首次亮相是作为一家独立公司长期任职的开始。取而代之的是，公共生活被巨大的支票所切断。这就是技术的“狗吃狗”的世界。
note_en:
Salesforce co-founder and CEO Marc Benioff didn’t mince words on his latest purchase. “This is a match made in heaven. Together, Salesforce and Slack will shape the future of enterprise software and transform the way everyone works in the all-digital, work-from-anywhere world,” Benioff said in a statement.
 Slack   CEO Stewart Butterfield was no less effusive than his future boss. “As software plays a more and more critical role in the performance of every organization, we share a vision of reduced complexity, increased power and flexibility, and ultimately a greater degree of alignment and organizational agility. Personally, I believe this is the most strategic combination in the history of software, and I can’t wait to get going,” Butterfield said in a statement.
  Every worker at every company needs to communicate, something that Slack can ably empower. What’s more, it also facilitates external communication with customers and partners, something that should be quite useful for a company like  Salesforce   and its family of offerings.
 Ultimately, Slack was ripe for the taking. Entering 2020 it had lost around 40% of its value since it went public. Consider that after its  most recent earnings report, the company lost 16% of its value, and before  the Salesforce deal leaked, the company was worth only a few dollars per share more than its direct listing reference price. Toss in net losses of $147.6 million during the two quarters ending July 31, 2020, Slack’s uninspiring public valuation and its winding path to profitability and it was a sitting target for a takeover like this one. The only surprise here is the price.
 Slack’s current valuation, according to both Yahoo and Google Finance is just over $25 billion, which given its very modest price change after-hours means that the market priced the company somewhat effectively. Slack is up around 48% from its valuation that preceded the deal becoming known.
  The new deal also puts Salesforce more on par —  and in competition — with its arch rival and sometime friend Microsoft, whose Teams product has been  directly challenging Slack in the market. Microsoft,  which passed on buying Slack in the past for a fraction of what Salesforce is paying today, has made Teams a key priority in recent quarters, loathe to cede any portion of the enterprise software market to another company.
 What really has set Slack apart from the pack, at least initially, was its ability to integrate with other enterprise software. When you combined that with bots, those intelligent digital helpers, the company could potentially provide Salesforce customers with a central place to work without changing focus because everything they need to do can be done in Slack.
  Today’s deal comes after Salesforce’s purchase of Quip in 2016 for $750 million. Quip brought a way of socially sharing documents to the SaaS giant, and when paired with the Slack acquisition gives Salesforce a much more robust social story to tell than its internal option Chatter, an early attempt at enterprise social that never really caught on.
 It’s worth noting that Salesforce was interested in Twitter in 2016, the same year that Microsoft was reportedly interested in Slack, but eventually walked away from that deal when shareholders objected, not wanting to deal with the controversial side of the social platform.
 Slack was founded in 2013, but  its origins go back to an online multiplayer game company called Glitch that was founded in 2009. While the game was ultimately a failure, the startup developed an internal messaging system in the process of building that company that later evolved into Slack.
 The company’s historic growth helped Slack raise over $1 billion while private,  earning an impressive $7 billion valuation before going public last year. But while the Glitch-to-unicorn story appears simple, Slack has always faced entrenched competition from the likes of not only Microsoft, but also Cisco, Facebook, Google and even Asana and Monday.com.
 For Slack, the path to the public markets was fraught with hype and outsized expectation. The company was famous, or as famous as an enterprise software company can be. At the time  it felt like the its debut was the start of a long tenure as an indie company. Instead, that public life has been cut short by a huge check. Such is the dog-eat-dog world of tech.
title: Salesforce在$ 27.7B Megadeal中购买Slack
title_en: Salesforce Buys Slack in a $27.7B Megadeal

该供稿位于Google Maps应用的“浏览”标签中，旨在让您更轻松地从可信任的本地来源中查找最新新闻，更新和推荐。这包括企业主使用“ Google我的商家”创建的帖子，以提醒客户新交易，菜单更新和其他优惠。谷歌表示，在发布会上，重点将放在突出食品和饮料企业的帖子上。
 多年来，企业已经可以使用Google的工具发布这类信息。但以前，用户必须专门点击以关注公司的资料，以接收其更新。
  现在，即使没有采取额外步骤关注特定业务的Google Maps用户也可以看到这些类似的帖子。谷歌说，增加的曝光率影响了帖子的浏览量。在社区Feed公开发布之前进行的早期测试中，Google发现，企业帖子中的观看次数是Feed出现之前的两倍以上。
  除了企业发布的信息外，新的社区供稿还将包含您关注的Google用户发布的内容以及Google本地向导的最新评论-志愿者计划，用户在该计划中分享自己对本地的知识，从而获得特殊待遇，例如个人资料徽章，抢先使用Google功能等等。某些发布商也会参与社区供稿，包括The Infatuation和Google新闻中的其他新闻来源（如果相关）。
 在今天发布之前，社区供稿中找到的许多信息都可以在Google地图的其他位置找到。
 例如，“ Google地图”的“更新”标签提供了类似的供稿，其中包括企业的帖子以及新闻，推荐，故事和旨在鼓励发现的其他功能。同时，“浏览”选项卡在屏幕顶部将业务分组为主题分组（例如，户外用餐场所，鸡尾酒吧等），然后允许用户浏览其他列表并查看区域照片。
 通过更新，按类别列出的那些业务组仍将位于屏幕顶部，但其余选项卡专用于可滚动的提要。这给选项卡带来了比以前更独特的感觉。鉴于TikTok样式的短视频提要的当前流行度，它甚至可能使Google将来涉足视频帖子，而Instagram和Snapchat现已克隆了这种提要。 
今天，它是一种更标准的供稿。向下滚动时，可以在您发现有趣的帖子上点击“赞”，以帮助更好地告知您将来的建议。您还可以在您想从中听到更多消息的企业上点击“关注”，这还将把他们的警报发送到“更新”选项卡。幸运的是，没有评论。
 Google希望这一更改将鼓励用户更多地访问该应用程序，以便了解他们所在的地区-无论是公司的新帖子还是其他用户的评论，其中详细介绍了一些有趣的本地活动，例如一日游或新例如，远足点。
 您也可以在旅行或研究其他地区时使用该功能，因为您看到的“社区摘要”不是根据您的居住地或当前位置来指定的，而是根据您在地图上的查看位置来指定的。
 该供稿是旨在使Google Maps更像Facebook竞争对手的一系列更新中的最新内容。在过去的几年中，Google Maps增加了一些功能，这些功能使用户可以像Facebook一样关注企业，并可以直接在应用中向这些企业发送消息，类似于Messenger。同时，企业已经能够在Google Maps中建立自己的个人资料，他们可以在其中添加徽标，封面照片并选择短名称-就像今天的Facebook Pages一样。
 随着新闻源样式功能的发布，Google试图复制Facebook的尝试变得更加明显。
 谷歌表示，该功能正在iOS和Android的Google Maps上全球推广。
note_en:
The feed, which is found with the Explore tab of the Google Maps app, is designed to make it easier to find the most recent news, updates, and recommendations from trusted local sources. This includes posts business owners create using Google My Business to alert customers to new deals, menu updates, and other offers. At launch, Google says the focus will be on highlighting posts from food and drink businesses.
 For years, businesses  have been able to make these sorts of posts using Google’s tools. But previously, users would have to specifically tap to follow the business’s profile in order to receive their updates.
  Now, these same sort of posts will be surfaced to even those Google Maps users who didn’t take the additional step of following a particular business. This increased exposure has impacted the posts’ views, Google says. In early tests of Community Feed ahead of its public launch, Google found that businesses’ posts saw more than double the number of views than before the feed existed.
  In addition to posts from businesses, the new Community Feed will feature content posted by  Google users you follow as well as recent reviews from  Google’s Local Guides — the volunteer program where users share their knowledge about local places in order to  earn perks, such as profile badges, early access to Google features, and more. Select publishers will participate in the Community Feed, too, including The Infatuation and other news sources from Google News, when relevant.
 Much of the information found in the Community Feed was available elsewhere in Google Maps before today’s launch.
 For example, the Google Maps’ Updates tab offered a similar feed that included businesses’ posts along with news, recommendations, stories, and other features designed to encourage discovery. Meanwhile, the Explore tab grouped businesses into thematic groupings (e.g. outdoor dining venues, cocktail bars, etc.) at the top of the screen, then allowed users to browse other lists and view area photos.
 With the update, those groups of businesses by category will still sit at the top of the screen, but the rest of the tab is dedicated to the scrollable feed. This gives the tab a more distinct feel than it had before. It could even position Google to venture into video posts in the future, given the current popularity of TikTok-style  short-form video feeds that have now cloned by Instagram and Snapchat.
  Today, it’s a more standard feed, however. As you scroll down, you can tap “Like” on those posts you find interesting to help better inform your future recommendations. You can also tap “Follow” on businesses you want to hear more from, which will send their alerts to your Updates tab, as well. Thankfully, there aren’t comments.
 Google hopes the change will encourage users to visit the app more often in order to find out what’s happening in their area — whether that’s a new post from a business or a review from another user detailing some fun local activity, like a day trip or new hiking spot, for example.
 The feature can be used when traveling or researching other areas, too, as the “Community Feed” you see is designated not based on where you live or your current location, but rather where you’re looking on the map.
 The feed is the latest in what’s been a series of updates designed to make Google Maps more of a Facebook rival. Over the past few years, Google Maps has added features that  allowed users to follow businesses, much like Facebook does, as well as  message those businesses directly in the app, similar to Messenger. Businesses, meanwhile, have been able to set up their  own profile in Google Maps, where they could add a logo, cover photo, and pick short name — also a lot like Facebook Pages offer today.
 With the launch of a news feed-style feature, Google’s attempt to copy Facebook is even more obvious.
 Google says the feature is rolling out globally on Google Maps for iOS and Android.
title: Google Maps通过推出自己的新闻提要而接受Facebook
title_en: Google Maps Takes on Facebook With Launch of Its Own News Feed

亚马逊正在推出廉价的新工具，这家科技巨头希望扩大其在工业领域的地位，从而使各地的工厂都能监控其工人和机器。
 由亚马逊云部门AWS推出的新的基于机器学习的服务包括监视重型机械运行状况的硬件，以及能够检测工人是否遵守社会疏离的计算机视觉。
 亚马逊表示，它已经创建了一个两英寸的低成本传感器Monitron，可以将其连接到设备上，以监视异常的振动或温度并预测未来的故障。
 同时，AWS Panorama是一项使用计算机视觉分析设施内摄像机收集的镜头的服务，可自动检测安全和合规性问题，例如不穿PPE的工人或在未经许可的区域驾驶的车辆。
  这项新服务于周二在公司年度云计算大会期间宣布，代表着这家科技巨头在目前认为服务不足的地区收集和处理现实数据的努力中的一个进步。
 “从总体上看制造业和工业，这是一个已经看到了一些创新的领域，但是其中很多作品尚未实现数字化和现代化，” AWS销售和营销负责人Matt Garman说。 ，与《金融时报》讲话。
 “工厂，制造设施或供应链中有大量数据。它只是固定在传感器中，固定在许多公司可以从中获得很多价值的机器中。” 
亚马逊表示，已在德国门兴格拉德巴赫附近的运营中心安装了1,000个Monitron传感器，用于监测输送带搬运包裹。
 Jefferies的分析师Brent Thill说，如果成功，这一举措将帮助亚马逊巩固其在云计算领域的主导地位，因为来自微软Azure和Google Cloud的竞争日益激烈，并且该领域的增长长期放缓。
 Thill先生说：“预测分析的想法可以超越工厂范围。”它可以进入汽车，桥梁或石油钻井平台。它可以使许多不同的产业交叉施肥。”
 许多公司已经在试用AWS Panorama。西门子移动表示，将使用该技术监控城市的交通流量，尽管未具体说明。德勤表示，它正在与北美一个主要海港合作，使用该工具来监控货运情况。
 但是，亚马逊自己使用的工具来监控员工的工作效率引起了批评者的关注。在整个大流行中，该公司一直使用计算机视觉来确保员工遵守社会疏离准则。
 AWS机器学习和AI负责人Swami Sivasubramanian表示，宣布的任何一项服务都不会包含“预包装”的面部识别功能，并表示AWS将阻止滥用其数据隐私和监控服务条款的客户。
 他告诉英国《金融时报》：“当您看一下这项技术时，有时我们很容易担心如何滥用它们。” 
“但是可以使用相同的技术来确保工人的安全。 人们在不应该去的地方行走吗？ 有漏油吗？ 他们不戴安全帽吗？ 这些是现实问题。”  ©2020金融时报有限公司。版权所有，不得以任何方式重新分发，复制或修改。
note_en:
Amazon is rolling out cheap new tools that will allow factories everywhere to monitor their workers and machines, as the tech giant looks to boost its presence in the industrial sector.
 Launched by Amazon’s cloud arm AWS, the new machine learning-based services include hardware to monitor the health of heavy machinery, and computer vision capable of detecting whether workers are complying with social distancing.
 Amazon said it had created a two-inch, low-cost sensor—Monitron—that can be attached to equipment to monitor abnormal vibrations or temperatures and predict future faults.
 AWS Panorama, meanwhile, is a service that uses computer vision to analyze footage gathered by cameras within facilities, automatically detecting safety and compliance issues such as workers not wearing PPE or vehicles being driven in unauthorized areas.
  The new services, announced on Tuesday during the company’s annual cloud computing conference, represent a step up in the tech giant’s efforts to gather and crunch real-world data in areas it currently feels are underserved.
 “If you look at manufacturing and industrial generally, it’s a space that has seen some innovations, but there's a lot of pieces that haven't been digitized and modernized,” said Matt Garman, AWS’s head of sales and marketing, speaking to the FT.
 “There's a ton of data in a factory, or manufacturing facility, or a supply chain. It's just locked up in sensors, locked up in machines that a lot of companies could get a lot of value from.”
    Amazon said it had installed 1,000 Monitron sensors at its fulfilment centres near the German city of Mönchengladbach, where they are used to monitor conveyor belts handling packages.
 If successful, said analyst Brent Thill from Jefferies, the move would help Amazon cement its position as the dominant player in cloud computing, in the face of growing competition from Microsoft’s Azure and Google Cloud, and a prolonged run of slowed segment growth.
 “This idea of predictive analytics can go beyond a factory floor,” Mr. Thill said. “It can go into a car, on to a bridge, or on to an oil rig. It can cross fertilize a lot of different industries.”
 A number of companies are already trialling AWS Panorama. Siemens Mobility said it would use the tech to monitor traffic flow in cities, though would not specify which. Deloitte said it was working with a major North America seaport to use the tool to monitor the movement of shipments.
 However Amazon’s own use of tools to monitor the productivity of employees has raised concerns among critics. Throughout the pandemic, the company has used computer vision to ensure employee compliance with social distancing guidelines.
 Swami Sivasubramanian, AWS’s head of machine learning and AI, said none of the services announced would include “pre-packaged” facial recognition capabilities, and said AWS would block clients who abused its terms of service on data privacy and surveillance.
 “When you look at this technology, sometimes it’s very easy for us to get worried about how they can be abused,” he told the FT.
 “But the same technology can be used to ensure worker safety. Are people walking in spaces where they shouldn’t be? Is there an oil spill? Are they not wearing hard hats? These are real-world problems.”

title: 亚马逊将推出监控工厂工人和机器的工具
title_en: Amazon To Roll Out Tools To Monitor Factory Workers and Machines
